title: ficus tinctoria subsp. parasitica
intro: Ficus tinctoria subsp. parasitica, also known as the dyer's fig, is a species of fig that belongs to the family Moraceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ficus tinctoria subsp. parasitica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
